audience: Online shop owners, e-commerce managers, marketers and manufacturers that need a consistent, scalable product image library.
primaryDutchSearchIntent: Webshopeigenaars helpen een volledige en herhaalbare productfotografieproductie voor SKU's en varianten plannen.

searchIntent.question: How should we plan repeatable product photography for all SKUs and variants, from shot list and colour control to feeds and accessible publication?
searchIntent.geographicContext: Online shops and brands, with professional production available from Limburg, Belgium.

longTailKeywords: product photography shot list for SKUs
directAnswer: Product photography for online shops works best as a repeatable catalogue process: define the question each image answers, create a tested specification by product family, track every view against its SKU and publish accurate responsive images with suitable alt text and product data.
title: Product photography for online shops: from shot list to product feed
metaDescription: Plan product photography with a repeatable shot list, consistent variants, accurate colour, clear naming and fast, accessible online shop images.
h1: Product photography for online shops: from shot list to product feed
factsToPreserve: Image types should answer distinct purchase questions and exact dimensions and compatibility remain product data rather than photographic claims.
Google Merchant Center main-image rules can change and should be checked for current feeds.
No photographer can guarantee identical colour on every screen.
Physical samples, image files and product records should use the same exact SKU.
Usage rights, model publication and third-party props require explicit scope and the article is not legal advice.
Alt text depends on image meaning and function, while redundant or decorative images may require empty alt text.
Clear images may support expectations but cannot guarantee fewer returns or higher sales.
